alu_to_load_ratio: ~55 vector-ALU ops per global load -> kernel body is dequant-compute, not load-bound
evidence: loads are already predominantly b128 (wide/coalesced) -> load width is NOT the bottleneck
kernel body dominated by integer nibble-unpack (shift/mask/cndmask/alignbit) + ubyte->f32 conversion + scale/min
both bandwidth (20-47% of peak) and implied ALU utilization are low -> latency/occupancy-bound on the long dequant dependency chain
small attn_q (less parallelism to hide dequant latency) sits at ~20% vs large ffn_gate at ~47%

real_search_axes: reduce dequant op count: lookup-table (LUT) 4-bit->fp dequant, bit-field-extract instead of shift+mask chains
vectorized unpack: more nibbles per instruction; fuse Q4_K scale/min application
raise occupancy / parallelism (esp. small matrices): more waves to hide the dequant dependency chain; tune parts

method: v1_partial DEBUG=7 instruction mix on attn_q (small, ~20% peak) and ffn_gate (large, ~47% peak)
note: Confirms why G0's ILP knobs (UPCAST/UNROLL) only hurt: they add register pressure to an already dequant/occupancy-bound kernel.
